source: .specs/agent-scenarios.md → Feature: Multiple agents in same workspace
reason: Requires a codex session running concurrently with a SECOND, different-adapter CLI (claudecode/pi/aider) in the same cwd. run-cell.sh takes a single <adapter> argument and dispatches to exactly one drive-<adapter>.sh driver; there is no flag, step type, or parallel mode that can launch + drive a foreign adapter alongside codex within one cell recording. Agent capability and irrlicht observability are both fine — see assessment.json (agent_supports=yes, irrlicht_observes=yes, confidence 0.85) — but no recording can be produced as-shipped. This cell is a dispatcher test that lives equally on the codex and claudecode rows of the matrix; once the driver supports paired adapters, the same fixture serves both rows.
notes: Spec preserved for forward compatibility: one codex session + one different-adapter session (e.g. claudecode) in the same cwd, each observed under its correct adapter label with independent state and metrics and no cross-contamination. Future phases (when the driver gains a cross-adapter parallel step that can route send/wait_turn to a named per-adapter target): codex_birth (adapter:codex, ready, new_session — codex mints its own rollout UUID under ~/.codex/sessions; session_id_assignment:false so it cannot be pre-pinned) and other_birth (adapter:claudecode, ready, distinct session under ~/.claude/projects/); per-adapter pid_bind (pid_discovered per session, distinct PIDs — codex binds via DiscoverPID, the PID holding its rollout open); codex_turn (ready→working→ready, same_session_as codex_birth) driven independently of other_turn (ready→working→ready, same_session_as other_birth); invariants on both turn windows: no state_transition leakage across the adapter boundary, no cross-contamination of model/tokens/cost, no parent linkage between the two sessions (asserted by adapter name on the SessionState row, not by shared cwd). Driver extension blocked: needs a concurrent-second-adapter primitive and matrix support for paired-CLI recipes — run-cell.sh drives a single adapter, no cross-adapter harness.
